title: URL Allowlisting, Untrusted API Data, Terminal Safety, Files, and Resource Abuse · Execution Trace: Pokedex Go Secret Privacy Logging
summary: The client follows redirects anywhere, prints control characters from remote data, accepts arbitrary save paths, logs full responses, and lets crafted records exhaust memory or terminal output. Predict the user decision, Go ownership, command state, API contract, resource budget, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex go secret privacy logging increment in deny-by-default Pokedex trust-boundary policy. Learners progress…
objectives: Avoid credentials, minimize retained data, redact diagnostic fields, and document that public API data can still become unsafe output.
Choose application-owned storage roots, reject traversal or unexpected file types, and minimize permission and symlink assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
